description_long: This object is a tall, cylindrical wine bottle with a slightly conical shoulder and a long, narrow neck. The body of the bottle is divided into two distinct sections: the upper portion is a dark, weathered metal with visible rust-like streaks, while the lower portion features a lighter, stone-like band with horizontal grooves. The bottle stands on a rectangular base, which appears to be made of the same dark metal as the upper body, and is connected to a protruding rectangular element, possibly a support or decorative feature. The surface of the bottle is matte and rough, giving it an industrial, aged appearance. No visible labels, branding, or decorative elements are present on the exterior.
description: A tall, weathered wine bottle with a dark metal upper body, a grooved stone-like lower band, and a rectangular base with a protruding support.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bottle's opening, the division between the dark upper and lighter lower sections, and the rectangular base with a protruding support facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length of the protruding rectangular support and the cylindrical profile of the bottle.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the continuous cylindrical body and the clear separation between the upper and lower materials, with the base and support less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the protruding support extending to the right, with the bottle's body and base visible.
attribute_bottle_neck_shape: long, narrow, straight neck with a gentle taper at the shoulder
